NUMEROUS REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING THROUGHOUT THE CITY OF MIAMI BEACH. LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT VEHICLES WERE STRANDED ALONG PORTIONS OF COLLINS RD... INDIANCREEK DRIVE... AND WASHINGTON STREET. THERE WERE ALSO NUMEROUS REPORTS OF FLOODING FROM SOCIAL MEDIA. RAINFALL REPORTS OF 4 TO 6 INCHES HAVE BEEN OBSERVED IN THIS AREA.

NUMEROUS REPORTS OF FLOOD WATERS ENTERING BUSINESSES... HOMES... APARTMENT LOBBIES AND PARKING GARAGES IN MIAMI BEACH AND SOUTH BEACH FROM SOCIAL MEDIA... AND TV NEWS MEDIA. WATER WAS 1 TO 2 FEET DEEP ON STREETS IN MANY AREAS OF SOUTH BEACH... INCLUDING PURDY AVE... WEST AVE... ALTON ROAD... PENNSYLVANIA AVE AND MERIDIAN AVE.

AT 900 PM TUESDAY AUGUST 1... OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS THAT MORE THAN 10 BUSINESSES... STORES... AND BUILDINGS IN THE VICINITY OF MARY BRICKELL VILLAGE HAD AT LEAST 1 TO 4 INCHES OF WATER INSIDE EARLIER IN THE AFTERNOON IN ASSOCIATION WITH THE HEAVY RAINS. THIS REPORT IS FROM WATER MARKS AND FROM SPEAKING WITH STORE EMPLOYEES. THIS INCLUDES STORES AND BUSINESSES ON SW 10TH STREET BETWEEN S MIAMI AVE AND SW 1ST AVE. FLASH FLOODING ALSO CLOSED SW 10TH STREET IN THIS AREA FOR HALF A BLOCK.

THERE WERE NUMEROUS REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING IN THE BRICKELL AREA OF DOWNTOWN MIAMI. LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS REPORTED NUMEROUS VEHICLES STRANDED IN THE AREA OF SW 1ST AVE AND 10TH ST AS WELL AS PORTIONS OF BRICKELL AVENUE.. RAINFALL REPORTS OF 3 TO 5 INCHES HAVE BEEN OBSERVED IN THIS AREA.
